name: Argamasilla de Alba from Ruidera – circular by La Solana and Castillo de Alhambra – El Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera
name_original: Argamasilla de Alba desde Ruidera – circular por La Solana y Castillo de Alhambra – El Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ruidera traverse a landscape defined by the Natural Park of Lagunas de Ruidera, a series of interconnected lakes and waterfalls. The terrain features a mix of open plains, agricultural areas, and sections alongside the lagoons. Road cyclists can expect varied gradients, from flat stretches to moderate ascents, offering diverse riding conditions. The region's physical features include the distinct lagoon system and surrounding natural areas. Best road cycling routes around Ruidera The most popular road cycling route is Argamasilla de Alba from Ruidera – circular by La Solana and Castillo de Alhambra – El Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera, a 61.7 miles (99.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes significant elevation gain and passes by historical sites. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Laguna del Rey – Access to La Isla loop from Laguna Redondilla, a moderate 52.8 miles (84.9 km) path. This route features varied terrain with notable elevation changes, offering views of the lagoons. Local road cyclists also love the El Hundimiento Waterfall – Laguna del Rey loop from Ruidera, a 25.0 miles (40.3 km) trail leading through the natural park, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Road cycling around Ruidera is defined by its lagoon system, open plains, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy road bike routes near Ruidera to more challenging moderate paths with elevation gain. The routes in Ruidera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ruidera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ruidera?
A: Yes, Ruidera offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera – Access to La Isla loop from Laguna Redondilla&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 15.4 km path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for those new to road cycling in the area.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of road cycling routes in Ruidera?
A: Road cycling routes in Ruidera vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter routes around 15 km, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera – Access to La Isla loop from Laguna Redondilla&lt;/a&gt; which takes about 35 minutes. Longer routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079774822/argamasilla-de-alba-from-ruidera-circular-by-la-solana-and-castillo-de-alhambra-el-parque-natural-de-las-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla de Alba from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to nearly 100 km and take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: Are there road cycling routes in Ruidera with significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: Yes, many routes in Ruidera offer notable elevation changes for a more challenging ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Access to La Isla – Montesinos Cave loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt; covers 92.7 km with approximately 788 meters of ascent, providing a good workout for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Ruidera?
A: The terrain around Ruidera is diverse, characterized by the Natural Park of Lagunas de Ruidera. You'll encounter a mix of open plains, agricultural areas, and sections running alongside the lagoons. Routes feature varied gradients, from flat stretches to moderate ascents, offering a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Ruidera?
A: While many routes are moderate, the easier options are generally suitable for families with some cycling experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Ruidera – Access to La Isla loop from Laguna Redondilla&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice, being shorter and less demanding, allowing families to enjoy the scenery together.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Ruidera?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for road biking in Ruidera. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during these months.

Q: What attractions or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes in Ruidera?
A: Road cycling routes in Ruidera often pass by stunning natural attractions. You can enjoy views of the interconnected lagoons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt; and various waterfalls, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5884091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hundimiento Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by historical sites or offer panoramic views of the natural park.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Ruidera?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Ruidera are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079774822/argamasilla-de-alba-from-ruidera-circular-by-la-solana-and-castillo-de-alhambra-el-parque-natural-de-las-lagunas-de-ruidera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla de Alba from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hundimiento Waterfall – Laguna del Rey loop from Ruidera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Ruidera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 10 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique landscape of the lagoon system, the varied gradients suitable for different skill levels, and the opportunity to combine cycling with natural sightseeing.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Ruidera region?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes documented in the Ruidera region, offering a range of difficulties from easy to moderate, and varying lengths to suit different preferences.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling trails?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, the routes often pass through or near towns and villages surrounding the lagoons, such as Ruidera itself. These locations typically offer opportunities for refreshments and breaks.

Q: Are there any wild swimming spots near the road bike trails in Ruidera?
A: The Natural Park of Lagunas de Ruidera is famous for its interconnected lakes, many of which are popular for swimming. While cycling, you'll pass by several lagoons where you might find designated swimming areas or spots suitable for a refreshing dip, such as around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The fortress originated as a Muslim fort, which would pass into Christian hands in 1198, remaining in the hands of the Order of San Juan in 1215, although it was conquered with the help of the Order of Santiago. Legend has it that after taking the fortress the image of Our Lady of Peñarroya was found, venerated since then by the residents of Argamasilla de Alba and La Solana, who share the Patron Saint.

This town in the province of Ciudad Real not only stands out for its Guadiana river and its Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, but also because this town is the famous place that Cervantes did not want to remember in his quintessential work, Don Quixote de La Mancha. .
The town of La Solana is located to the southeast of the province of iudad Real, in a flat area with gentle hills, except for the heights of the Sierra de Alhambra located to the south.

You absolutely must visit this unique and striking castle. I recommend climbing to the top to enjoy the views from its privileged elevated position and imagining the times when such thick walls were necessary.

The King's Lagoon, one of the most emblematic lagoons in the Ruidera Lakes Natural Park:
It is located within the municipality of Ruidera (Ciudad Real), at the main entrance to the natural park.

It is one of the first lagoons you see when traveling through the park by road, very close to the town of Ruidera.
It stands out for the turquoise color of its waters, which in some places are reminiscent of the Caribbean.
It is photogenic and easily accessible, ideal for those looking for a place to relax or take a swim.
The bottom is clearly visible thanks to the water's transparency.

waypoints.name: View of Peñarroya Castle and Reservoir
